title: Boulder Co Vlog 4/6/18: Roller Rode 50 Mi 3Hrs, finished taxes

enrichment.summary: Kyle recaps his April 6th day: he slept poorly for the second night in a row, spent the day at his mom's house doing taxes (which cost around $360 through TurboTax), then came home and rode his bike on rollers for three hours, covering about 50 miles with a suffer score of 161. He reflects on his incremental training approach inspired by NASA's baby-steps philosophy and expresses excitement about his current solo training environment.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Believes in baby steps rather than jumping to maximum effort, inspired by NASA's methodical approach
Excited that living alone with no distractions is the ideal situation for serious training, unlike high school
Thinks ~$360 for TurboTax is expensive and not cheaper than H&R Block as he expected

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I rode my bike for 70 miles and got a suffer score of 161, which is extreme.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I was up all night and didn't sleep at all the night before.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I set my alarm for 5 a.m. but only slept until 3 a.m.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I went to my mom's house to do tax stuff, which took all day.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I rode for three hours today, the longest I've ridden on rollers.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I've been watching NASA videos and learning about the moon landing.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm taking baby steps in my training to keep it comfortable.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I felt stronger during my ride today.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm proud of my current state of 143 watts.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to do a 20-minute power test soon.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I've been listening to music while making videos at my mom's house.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I decided not to do my history tests as planned.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm recording my bike rides and watts to track progress.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm training in a way I've never tried before, focusing on consistency.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm excited about my current training routine because I have no distractions.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I haven't been taking advantage of my time to ride, but now I can.

transcript.text: Hello, living in Boulder V-Log.

I get a suffer score of 161, which is extreme, according to Strava.
But I wanted to ride today. I felt like I could ride today.
But the problem was, I was up all night.
I thought I was going to be able to sleep last night, because I didn't sleep at all the night before last night.
So, I didn't anticipate that I'd be up for two nights in a row, but I was up the entire night.
I set my alarm for 5. I want to set my alarm for 5 one night.
Or even earlier, when I go riding, because you have to have breakfast.
And then by the time you get out, even if it's before the sun rises, it's light enough.

So, I guess I have to set the alarm for 4.40 or something.
But anyway, so yeah, I slept until like 3.
Because by then, I was gone, and I was tired.

What's awesome about training all the time is the rest is amazing.
So, I got up, and I called my mom, because I wanted to do this tax stuff out of the way.

I think they're not cheaper than H&R Block anymore.

We were just hanging out and doing that, and made a fire, because it was snowing today.
Okay, then I came home and decided that was a good time to ride.

I've been riding two hours.
I did two hours twice, two hours once.
I've been riding for like a week or something.
And yesterday, I rode four and a half hours.
So, today, three hours, I, you know, maybe could have done four hours today, but I like doing it in increments.
I never would have been like this before.
I would have just wanted to jump right into it.
But, I've been watching NASA videos, and learning about this, you know, the moon landings and stuff.
And they did all that stuff in baby steps, you know.
Like, they wouldn't do, they wouldn't skip anything.

Just baby steps, just to keep it comfortable.
